channel.title: Essays on Semih Berkay Öztürk
channel.description: Recent content in Essays on Semih Berkay Öztürk

item.title: Putting a model in charge of KV-cache memory
item.description: I’ve been reading through how vLLM, SGLang, and NVIDIA’s Dynamo handle KV-cache when memory gets tight. The papers are clear enough, but I didn’t really understand the tradeoffs until I tried to turn them into code. So I built a small RL environment around the part of the problem I cared about: the inference server is filling up, requests are queueing, and something has to decide what to evict, compress, or swap before the system falls behind.

item.title: Speeding up diffusion models with first block caching
item.description:
Inference speed and quality is all you need from a diffusion model and one of the simplest but very effective optimization techniques is something called “first block caching” that can significantly speed up inference with minimal quality loss.

The main idea

The idea behind first block caching is dead simple: not every timestep in a diffusion model’s denoising process requires the same amount of computation. Some steps produce large changes to the latent representation, while others make only minor adjustments. By detecting when a timestep will produce minimal changes, we can skip most of the computation for that step.
